sub_stages.name: Confidence and Over-Reliance on Past Success

selection_logic.dominant_stage_rule: Pick stage with highest own-stage score
selection_logic.dominant_substage_rule: Pick substage with highest own-substage score inside dominant stage

strengths: Ability to maintain stability while adapting to change.
Strong alignment with organizational goals and execution consistency.
Collaborative mindset that supports team coordination and trust.
Established processes and structure that improve predictability and control.
weaknesses: Risk of over-comfort and subtle complacency in stable operations.
Can struggle to prioritize rapidly when conditions change suddenly.
Innovation speed may reduce if too dependent on existing systems.
May delay improvement initiatives due to focus on stability.
opportunities: Opportunity to strengthen agility while keeping operational discipline.
Chance to scale performance through continuous optimization.
Ability to lead by example in stable + adaptable execution.
Room to improve resilience and efficiency through smarter processes.
threats: Unexpected disruption can impact predictability if response is slow.
Over-extension can cause burnout when adapting too frequently.
Lack of proactive innovation may cause competitive disadvantage.
Misalignment during transitions can reduce stakeholder trust.

framework.name: Dynamic Balance

recommendation_blocks.title: Leverage Your Strengths
recommendation_blocks.description: Maintain your operational stability while using your strong structure and alignment to respond faster to changes. Your ability to stay calm and coordinated is a big advantage during shifting business environments.

recommendation_blocks.title: Mitigate Weaknesses
recommendation_blocks.description: Avoid subtle complacency by setting short review cycles. Improve prioritization by defining what matters most each week and ensuring execution stays aligned with goals even when new challenges appear.

recommendation_blocks.title: Seize Opportunities
recommendation_blocks.description: Use this stable phase to build agility through small experiments, process improvements, and capability building. Stability is your base—now use it to grow faster and smarter.

recommendation_blocks.title: Address Threats
recommendation_blocks.description: Prepare for disruption with proactive planning and contingency thinking. Strengthen stakeholder communication to ensure alignment and trust during any operational shifts.

recommendation_blocks.title: Balanced Growth Recommendation
recommendation_blocks.description: The best strategy is to protect stability, but operate with flexibility—moving quickly when needed while keeping long-term focus intact.

actionable_steps: Run a weekly stability check: what is stable, what is changing, and what needs attention.
Define top 3 priorities every week and assign clear ownership for each task.
Use short pilots to test improvements before full implementation.
Review progress every 7 days and adjust execution plan based on results.
Strengthen communication: weekly updates to stakeholders on key shifts and decisions.
Track key performance metrics (quality, speed, output consistency) to prevent drift.
Invest time in one improvement initiative monthly (automation/process optimization).
Create a contingency checklist for unexpected operational changes.
Encourage team feedback loops to catch issues early.
Celebrate wins to reinforce a culture of progress and adaptability.

final_report_output.summary: This dimension strongly reflects a Steady-State pattern with Dynamic Balance as the dominant substage. The candidate is stable, structured, and operationally consistent, while also capable of adapting to change. The key improvement area is to avoid subtle complacency and convert stability into proactive agility.
recommended_focus_next_14_days: Maintain stability but improve responsiveness using weekly priority alignment.
Start 1 improvement pilot to increase speed or efficiency.
Strengthen stakeholder communication and decision clarity.
final_report_output.final_recommendation: Continue operating from stability, but introduce controlled change cycles (weekly reviews + small experiments) to sustain long-term growth without losing alignment.
